title: Secreted dual reporter assay with Gaussia luciferase and mCherry
author: Diana Wider
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kbwcspe

steps.title: Transfer 100 µl of supernatant into...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µl of supernatant into 96-well dish (black flat bottom, non-binding surface polysterene; e.g. Corning catalog number 3991).

steps.title: Measure the mCherry fluorescence in a...
steps.instruction: Measure the mCherry fluorescence in a fluorometer (see Guidelines for detailed instrument settings).

steps.title: Add 50 µl Gluc buffer per...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl Gluc buffer per well to trigger the Gluc bioluminescence reaction. Since the luminescence gradually declines (to about 80% by 6 minutes; see Maguire et al., 2009, Anal. Chem. 81, 7102), it is important to work rapidly and beyond a certain number of wells, to use a multichannel pipet (or injector).

steps.title: If you need to measure a...
steps.instruction: If you need to measure a more diluted sample, dilute supernatants separately with Opti-MEM and Opti-MEM complemented with 10% FBS for mCherry and Gluc, respectively.
